sketch-what-is-concat.title: What is Text Concatenation?
sketch-what-is-concat.bodyMarkdown:
Text concatenation means joining two or more text values together into a single string. In SQL, you can combine columns or literal text using the concatenation operator. In SQLite, the operator for concatenation is `||` (double pipe). For example, to join the `full_name` and `city` columns from the `customers` table:

```sql
SELECT full_name || ', ' || city AS name_and_city FROM customers;
```
This will produce results like `Mia Lopez, Chicago`.

sketch-concat-with-literals.title: Concatenating Columns and Literal Text
sketch-concat-with-literals.bodyMarkdown:
You can concatenate columns with literal strings to format your output. For example, to create a label that says "Customer: [Name] from [City]":

```sql
SELECT 'Customer: ' || full_name || ' from ' || city AS label FROM customers;
```
If a column is NULL, the result of the concatenation will also be NULL.

sketch-null-concat.title: Handling NULLs in Concatenation
sketch-null-concat.bodyMarkdown:
When concatenating, if any value is NULL, the entire result becomes NULL. For example:

```sql
SELECT full_name || ' (' || email || ')' AS contact_info FROM customers;
```
If `email` is NULL, the result will be NULL for that row. To avoid this, you can use the `COALESCE()` function to provide a fallback value:

```sql
SELECT full_name || ' (' || COALESCE(email, 'no email') || ')' AS contact_info FROM customers;
```

quiz-single-choice-concat-operator.title: Concatenation Operator in SQLite
quiz-single-choice-concat-operator.prompt: Which operator is used to concatenate text values in SQLite?

help.concept: SQLite uses a specific operator to join text values together. This operator is different from addition or bitwise operators.
help.hint_1: Look for the operator that is two identical symbols.
help.hint_2: The operator is two vertical bars placed together.

quiz-multi-choice-concat-usage.title: Valid Ways to Concatenate in SQLite
quiz-multi-choice-concat-usage.prompt: Which of the following are valid ways to concatenate the `full_name` and `city` columns in SQLite?
quiz-multi-choice-concat-usage.hint: Remember the correct operator and syntax for SQLite.

help.concept: SQLite uses the `||` operator for concatenation, not a function or the plus sign.
help.hint_1: Check which options use the double pipe operator.
help.hint_2: Options with `||` are correct; SQLite does not have a built-in CONCAT function.

quiz-drag-reorder-concat-syntax.title: Build a Concatenation Expression
quiz-drag-reorder-concat-syntax.prompt: Arrange the tokens to form a valid SQLite expression that combines `full_name` and `city` with a comma and space between them.
quiz-drag-reorder-concat-syntax.hint: Start with the first column, then the operator, then the literal, and so on.

help.concept: Concatenation in SQLite is done by chaining columns and literals with the `||` operator.
help.hint_1: The pattern is: column, operator, literal, operator, column.
help.hint_2: Place the literal between the two columns using `||` on both sides.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-null-concat.title: Effect of NULL in Concatenation
quiz-fill-blank-choice-null-concat.prompt: If you concatenate `full_name` and `email` and the `email` value is NULL, what will the result be?
quiz-fill-blank-choice-null-concat.hint: What happens in SQLite when any value in a concatenation is NULL?

help.concept: In SQLite, concatenating any value with NULL results in NULL.
help.hint_1: NULL in any part of the concatenation makes the whole result NULL.
help.hint_2: The output will not show the name or a string, but will be NULL.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-null-concat.template: SELECT full_name || ' - ' || email AS contact FROM customers;
choices: The result is just the full_name
The result is an empty string

quiz-code-input-concat-label.title: Write a Concatenation Query
quiz-code-input-concat-label.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all customers, showing a column called `label` that combines `full_name` and `city` in the format: `[full_name] from [city]`.
quiz-code-input-concat-label.hint: Use the `||` operator and include the literal text ' from '.

help.concept: Concatenate columns and literal text using the `||` operator in SQLite.
help.hint_1: Combine `full_name`, the string ' from ', and `city` using `||`.
help.hint_2: The format should be: full_name, then ' from ', then city, all joined with `||`.
